name: Kitchen knives
summary: Chef knives, santokus, and knife sets for home cooking.

evidence.claim: ASTM E18-22 'Standard Test Methods for Rockwell Hardness of Metallic Materials' is the recognized US standard for HRC measurement, used by knife manufacturers to certify steel hardness claims.
Chef Knives To Go reference on common kitchen-knife steel hardness ranges.

criteria.name: steel_hardness_hrc
criteria.notes: Japanese 60-64 holds edge longer; German/Western 55-58 more forgiving (E1).

confabulationPatterns.reality: Modern 'Damascus' is typically pattern-welded cladding over a mono-steel core; the pattern is decorative. Edge performance depends on the core steel, not the cladding.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: What is the core steel composition? Pattern is aesthetic, not performance.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Vague claim. 'Japanese steel' can mean VG-10, AUS-8, AUS-10, or others with very different properties. Specific steel matters far more than 'Japanese.'
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: What is the specific steel alloy (VG-10, AUS-10, SG2, etc.)?

compatibilityQuestions: Right or left-handed? Some Japanese knives have single-bevel edges handed.
Do you maintain a whetstone? Harder steels require more skill to sharpen.

repairability.notes: Professional sharpening services can resurrect most knives.
